summary: Marathon Eyes>Foolish, soaring Might As Well, second‑set fireworks
blurb: The show rides a high‑energy wave from the epic Eyes Of The World → Foolish Heart transition into a blistering first‑set close with Might As Well. A nonstop second set carries the momentum through Estimated Prophet, The Other One and a seamless Wharf Rat → Sugar Magnolia finale.
review: On March 20, 1991 at the Capital Centre, the Dead delivered one of those rare nights where everything clicked. Jerry was in an especially good mood, his guitar soaring on the marathon Eyes Of The World > Foolish Heart segue and driving the band into a joyous, “hot” drum‑and‑space segment that kept the crowd buzzing. The first set built strong momentum, highlighted by a powerful Might As Well that sent the audience into a frenzy. The second set never let up—Estimated Prophet flowed into He’s Gone, then a thunderous Drums/Space burst gave way to an electrifying run through The Other One, Wharf Rat and Sugar Magnolia that felt like it could have gone on forever. Keyboard textures from both Bruce Hornsby and Vince Welnick added extra sparkle, even if some listeners found the mix dense. Despite a few technical glitches (notably a missing encore segment on one tape), the overall performance is consistently praised across all recordings and stands as a high‑water mark for the 1991 tour.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All six recording analyses carry a positive sentiment, repeatedly praising the Eyes Of The World > Foolish Heart marathon, the soaring Might As Well, and the nonstop second‑set energy. Multiple sources highlight the same standout songs and describe the band as "locked in" and "electrifying," indicating strong consensus of excellence.

best_recording.reason: Solid soundboard capture with clear mix and full set coverage; despite occasional glitches it delivers the most complete listening experience.

key_highlights: Epic Eyes Of The World → Foolish Heart transition that drives the show.
"Might As Well" closes the first set with soaring energy.
Second‑set jam marathon: Estimated Prophet into He’s Gone, then The Other One → Wharf Rat → Sugar Magnolia.
Hot drumming and a powerful drum‑and‑space segment adds excitement throughout.

band_performance.Jerry: In an especially good mood, Garcia delivers powerhouse guitar on the Eyes > Foolish transition and fuels the band's high energy throughout.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh provides a solid bass foundation; his only noted slip‑up occurs during the missing encore segment.
band_performance.Bob: Weir offers strong vocal work and shares an engaging onstage chat with Jerry before Might As Well.
band_performance.Vince: Keyboard contributions are energetic, lifting the Eyes > Foolish and Might As Well sections despite some listeners finding the mix dense.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums are praised as hot, especially during the drum‑and‑space segment that adds excitement.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann’s drumming matches Mickey’s intensity, helping drive the nonstop second‑set flow.
